content: Heart failure is a chronic condition where the heart cannot pump blood effectively. Post-discharge management includes: 1) Daily weight monitoring - weigh yourself at the same time each day and report weight gain of 2-3 pounds in one day or 5 pounds in one week. 2) Medication adherence - take all prescribed medications as directed, including ACE inhibitors, beta-blockers, and diuretics. 3) Fluid restriction - limit fluid intake to 1.5-2 liters per day as recommended by your healthcare provider. 4) Sodium restriction - limit sodium intake to less than 2000mg per day. 5) Physical activity - engage in light to moderate exercise as tolerated, with rest periods. 6) Monitor symptoms - watch for increased shortness of breath, swelling in legs or ankles, persistent cough, or fatigue. Contact your healthcare provider immediately if symptoms worsen.

citationFormat.citationText: American Heart Association. (2023). Heart Failure Management Guidelines. Retrieved from https://www.heart.org/en/health-topics/heart-failure
